description: Fix broken CSS, Firebase Auth API key errors, and verify friend flow in beta and production environments

userStories.title: Investigate and fix broken CSS in beta
userStories.description: As a user, I want the beta web app to render with proper Tailwind CSS styling so that it looks the same as localhost.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate why CSS is broken — likely causes: Tailwind v4 + Next.js standalone output not including CSS, Docker build not processing PostCSS, or static assets not being copied in the Dockerfile
Identify root cause and apply fix (may involve Dockerfile changes, next.config.mjs, or PostCSS/Tailwind config)
Redeploy to beta
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: beta web URL (https://buddyburn-beta-web.gentletree-d517e76a.eastus.azurecontainerapps.io) renders with proper styling (orange primary buttons, proper layout, font sizing) matching localhost
userStories.notes: Tailwind v4 uses @import 'tailwindcss' and @tailwindcss/postcss plugin. Next.js standalone output copies server.js but static assets (including CSS in .next/static) must be explicitly copied in the Dockerfile. Check apps/web/Dockerfile and next.config.mjs output setting. The deploy workflow is .github/workflows/deploy-web.yml. VERIFIED: CSS is already working correctly in beta — matches localhost styling.

userStories.title: Investigate and fix Firebase API key error in beta
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in to the beta web app without seeing 'Error (auth/api-key-not-valid.-please-pass-a-valid-api-key.)'.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate root cause — likely causes: NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY build arg not set or set to wrong value in deploy-web workflow, GitHub environment secrets not configured for beta, or Key Vault secret not populated
Verify that the beta GitHub Actions environment has the correct NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY secret (this value is baked into the Next.js build at Docker image build time)
Redeploy to beta with the correct Firebase config
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: beta login page loads FirebaseUI widget without API key errors
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: a test user can sign in successfully
userStories.notes: FIXED: Firebase Web SDK config stored in Azure Key Vault (firebase-web-* secrets) for both beta and prod. Deploy workflow updated to fetch from KV with fallback to GitHub secrets. Deployed via workflow run 22659058861 — all jobs passed including E2E tests. Verified: beta login page renders FirebaseUI widget, 'Sign in with email' flow shows Create Account form with no API key errors. JS bundle now has real API key (AIzaSy...) instead of demo-api-key.

userStories.title: Fix broken CSS in production
userStories.description: As a user, I want the production web app to render with proper Tailwind CSS styling, matching the fix applied to beta.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the same CSS fix from US-001 to production (deploy the same image or trigger prod deploy)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: production web URL (https://buddyburn-prod-web.bravepebble-01e00c04.eastus.azurecontainerapps.io) renders with proper styling matching localhost and beta
userStories.notes: VERIFIED: Production CSS is already working correctly. The same CSS bundle (6ce528821d89d1ff.css) loads on both beta and prod, rendering identical orange gradient title, proper fonts, and centered layout. No code changes were needed — the Dockerfile and Next.js standalone config already handle static assets correctly.

userStories.title: Fix Firebase API key error in production
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in to the production web app without Firebase API key errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the same Firebase config fix from US-002 to production
Verify that the production GitHub Actions environment has the correct NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY secret
Redeploy to production
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: production login page (https://buddyburn-prod-web.bravepebble-01e00c04.eastus.azurecontainerapps.io/login) loads without errors and a test user can sign in
userStories.notes: FIXED: Same deploy workflow run 22659058861 deployed to both beta and production. Production KV (buddyburn-prod-kv) has identical firebase-web-* secrets. All 5 workflow jobs passed including Deploy Web to Production and E2E Tests (Production). Production login page works without API key errors.

userStories.title: Verify add-a-friend flow in beta
userStories.description: As a user, I want to verify that the add-a-friend happy path works correctly in the beta environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: navigate to Friends page in beta (https://buddyburn-beta-web.gentletree-d517e76a.eastus.azurecontainerapps.io/friends)
Click '+ Add Friend' button, search for a user by email, confirm the search result appears
Click the found user, confirm the 'Send Friend Request?' dialog appears
Send the friend request, confirm it appears under 'Pending Requests' as outgoing
Log in as the other user, confirm the incoming request appears, click 'Accept'
Verify both users now appear in each other's Friends list
Document any issues found in notes (proceed to US-006 if broken)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: VERIFIED (with issues found — proceed to US-006). Two test users created: alice.test@burnbuddy.dev and bob.test@burnbuddy.dev (password: BurnBuddy2026!). Issues found: (1) API auth broken — FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID not set on API container, defaults to 'burnbuddy-dev' but actual Firebase project is 'patelr3-site' (confirmed via JWT aud claim). All API calls return 401 'Invalid or expired token'. (2) Friends page shows 'Failed to load friends' due to auth failure. (3) User search returns 'No user found' due to auth failure. (4) FirebaseUI email enumeration protection prevents re-login — shows 'Create account' for existing users and 'email already exists without any means of sign-in' when trying. (5) firebase-service-account-json in KV has project_id:'buddyburn-beta' which doesn't match actual project 'patelr3-site'. Fix in US-006: set FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=patelr3-site on API container and update service account JSON.

userStories.title: Fix friend flow issues if any
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to fix any issues discovered in US-005 so that the add-a-friend flow works end-to-end in beta.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix all issues identified in US-005 (skip this story if US-005 passes cleanly)
Typecheck passes
API tests pass (cd services/api && yarn test)
Re-verify the full friend flow in beta using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005. Issues to fix from US-005: (1) CRITICAL: API container missing FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID env var — defaults to 'burnbuddy-dev' but must be 'patelr3-site' (the actual Firebase project). Fix: add FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID to API container env vars via Bicep/deploy workflow. (2) firebase-service-account-json in KV has wrong project_id 'buddyburn-beta' — should be 'patelr3-site'. (3) FirebaseUI email enumeration protection causes re-login issues. (4) API firebase.ts code checks for FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT env var but container has FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON. Friend-related API routes are in services/api/src/routes/friends.ts. Friend-related web pages are in apps/web/src/app/friends/page.tsx.
